items.title: Paedophile ex-magistrate Peter Liddy leaves prison after 25 years

children.content: Notorious paedophile ex-magistrate Peter Liddy has taken his first steps of freedom after serving a 25-year prison term for the sexual abuse of four children.

articles.title: Paedophile magistrate's release prompts vigilante warning

children.content: South Australia's police chief says the force will respond accordingly to concerns about public safety once paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy is released from prison, but added there would be no tolerance for "vigilante activity".

image.altText: Notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy.

gallery.altText: A man in a black hat and black jacket walks through a gate with a brick building behind,

thumbnail.altText: A man in a black hat and black jacket walks through a gate with a brick building behind,

items.title: Heatwaves are becoming so extreme, they're 'breaking the biology' of plants

children.content: Record-breaking heatwaves decimated crops in Australia this year and served as a "real wake-up call" for the urgent need to develop more heat-resistant crops and research into food security.

image.altText: a generic photo of a wheat crop

items.title: Police search 1,000-plus outback mine shafts and dam for missing mother

children.content: After three days of searching, South Australian police are no closer to finding out what happened to missing mother of two Trisha Graf.

image.altText: Trisha Graf search

items.title: 'Infuriating': Aboriginal voters turned away, told to line-up again on SA election day

children.content: South Australia's Electoral Commission was aware that forcing First Nations voters to queue twice for the Voice election would be perceived as "discriminatory behaviour". Despite that, voters say it still happened and, in some cases, they were turned away.

articles.title: Poor advertising, confused voters and underfunded candidates: SA Voice's problems revealed

children.content: New documents suggest South Australia's inaugural Voice to Parliament election was hamstrung by advertising failures that left First Nations voters confused or unaware an election was taking place.

image.altText: Scott Wilson looks at the camera with a serious look on his face. He has grey hair, a full beard, and is wearing a black jacket.

gallery.altText: A woman wearing a maroon shirt and checked cardigan looks over her right shoulder.

thumbnail.altText: A woman wearing a maroon shirt and checked cardigan looks over her right shoulder.

items.title: 15yo girl left with fractured skull after e-scooter crash

children.content: Two separate e-device accidents, one of which left a 15-year-old girl with a fractured skull, has raised "genuine" concern about the use of such devices a month out of a planned SA government review.

image.altText: E bikes lined up in a row inside a bike shop

items.title: 'Too dangerous to push ahead': Aussie diver leaves Laos cave rescue

children.content: Josh Richards is on his way back to Australia, after multiple days helping rescue miners trapped in a Laos cave.

image.altText: A man with a beard and cap in a jungle camp

items.title: SA budget to include new treaty money, public service cuts

children.content: Cuts to South Australia's public service and new funding for a treaty commission have been announced as the state Labor government gets set to reveal its budget on Thursday.

gallery.altText: A man with short grey and black hair wearing a suit and tie speaking in front of microphones

thumbnail.altText: A man with short grey and black hair wearing a suit and tie speaking in front of microphones

items.title: Bronze statue immortalises Aboriginal leader Lowitja O'Donoghue

children.content: A bronze statue of influential Aboriginal leader Lowitja O'Donoghue has been unveiled in Adelaide — the first of six monuments of prominent Indigenous South Australians that the state government has planned.

image.altText: A  bronze statue of a woman who holds her hands at the front of her body.

items.title: How one optimal AUKUS submarine pathway became two

children.content: Scrutiny of AUKUS's delivery reveals two "optimal pathways" for Australia's submarines.

image.altText: A virginia class submarine above water, with naval officers standing on top. Two boat ships are nearby.

items.title: Sex offender evicted after women footballers abandon bush club

children.content: A south-west Victorian football club has apologised after it allowed a convicted sex offender to return to the club after a stint in jail.

image.altText: A Tyrendarra Football Netball Club training jumper can be seen on a timber bench.

items.title: Sheepskins go from being worthless to in demand in a year

children.content: Just 12 months ago, Australian sheepskins were worthless and being dumped in landfill. They are now in hot demand amid a surging merino wool industry.

image.altText: Pink and purple ugg boots sit on a shelf.

items.title: One Nation SA says it will not support 'one scrap of legislation' by Labor

children.content: South Australia's One Nation has vowed to block every Labor government bill following a dispute over staffing allocations.

image.altText: composite of Kyam Maher and Cory Bernardi both looking serious

items.title: Expensive watches, work bags and gold-plated stationery claimed in SA Health scheme

children.content: The Independent Commission Against Corruption (ICAC) has called for stronger governance of professional development reimbursements for public sector medical officers, with some staff making claims for luxury items like watches and work bags.

image.altText: A watch with a digital screen on a man's wrist with numbers on it.

items.title: Inquest considers whether care of Adelaide man's bed sores was 'appropriate and reasonable'

children.content: A coronial inquest will investigate whether an aged care facility in Adelaide's inner north appropriately treated the bed sores a 79-year-old man with dementia developed in the lead-up to his death.

image.altText: The Coroners Court sign in Adelaide.

items.title: Quicksand causes terrifying ‘Indiana Jones moment’ for second Adelaide beachgoer

children.content: Days after a young Adelaide beachgoer had to be rescued from quicksand, a second person has spoken out about a similar experience along the same stretch of coast, saying she was momentarily overcome by "complete fear".

image.altText: A woman in a raincoat cuddling her dog.

items.title: British PM urges calm after protests over handcuffed Henry Nowak's death

children.content: Video of officers ignoring the pleas of a dying, innocent man has sparked a political storm about how police treat ‌different ethnicities.

image.altText: UK Police officers in yellow hi-viz clothing, helmets and behind riot shields clouded in red flare smoke.

items.title: 'A little perturbed': Trump confirms expletive-laden call with Netanyahu

children.content: In an interview with a US podcast, the US president says he was frustrated that Benjamin Netanyahu was "constantly fighting with Lebanon".

image.altText: Donald Trump gestures next to Benjamin Netanyahu who holds his thumbs up.

items.title: Australia joins exclusive club granting access to advanced AI

children.content: Fresh access to AI deemed too dangerous for public release has ramped up pressure on the government to respond to possible risks.

articles.title: Economic report says 82 occupations have been 'AI-disrupted'

children.content: A new economic report by consultancy firm Deloitte says the AI "winners" will be organisations that combine human and machine strengths, with recruiters saying human judgement will still be key.

image.altText: A woman in a white shirt and long hair looks at camera.

articles.title: What's got the pope and most Australians worried? This hyped new technology

children.content: Most Australians appear to distrust and dislike artificial intelligence despite using technology more than people in other countries.

image.altText: A close up of Pope Leo.

gallery.altText: Anthropic logo is seen in this photo

thumbnail.altText: Anthropic logo is seen in this photo

items.title: Cannabis driving limit change to give 'freedom' to medicinal users in NSW

children.content: The Labor government plans to introduce a legal limit on the concentration of THC a driver can have in their system if they have a prescription.

image.altText: Police officers testing drivers for cannabis near Nimbin

items.title: ASX falls on renewed Middle East tensions while SpaceX locked for IPO

children.content: The ASX has fallen by more than 1 per cent following Wall Street slipping overnight as hopes for a swift end to the war in Iran faded again. Follow the latest on our live markets blog.

image.altText: An ASX board shot by John Gunn in Deccember 2022.

items.title: Ban on travelling under bridge being reviewed amid refuelling debacle

children.content: Authorities are considering whether it is feasible for Australia's Antarctic icebreaker to safely travel under Hobart's Tasman Bridge, as the Tasmanian government tries to resolve a costly refuelling saga.

gallery.altText: A large orange ship approaches a bridge span

thumbnail.altText: A large orange ship approaches a bridge span

items.title: Sportsbet's owner walks through 'wide open gate' in gambling laws

children.content: One of the world's biggest gambling companies is breaking into the 'rewards club' scene, raising the alarm among anti-gambling advocates.

image.altText: Two AFL players on a field, a sportsbet logo in the background.

items.title: World number one gutted after 'dirty' loss in French Open quarterfinal

children.content: Aryna Sabalenka feels the pain of a shock loss in the quarterfinals of the French Open, saying she just feels like smashing things.

image.altText: Aryna Sabalenka pulls a face as she carries her bags off court at the French Open.

items.title: How to move forward from estrangement

children.content: Experts say healing might involve reducing emotional distress and gaining clarity from the situation.

gallery.altText: Two people sitting on a park bench with their backs to the camera

thumbnail.altText: Two people sitting on a park bench with their backs to the camera

items.title: 'A huge gamble': How design shows choose their projects

children.content: A planned 12-month build that's still underway three years later is the running joke of design shows. But what happens when you're a TV producer trying to make a series of it?

image.altText: A camera films an old building with scaffolding around it.

items.title: Quiz: Can you recognise these 10 classic Australian album covers?

children.content: Take a closer look at some of the biggest albums in Australian music and tell us what you see.

image.altText: Australian singer-songwriter Missy Higgins sits in a blue satin dress, arms folded, against black backdrop

items.title: A year since the varroa mite entered Qld, honey bee colonies have collapsed

children.content: Beekeepers in Queensland say they are 'barely hanging on' a year after the varroa mite was detected in the state for the first time.
